content_of_post_to_answer: How do I overcome the past, and how do I stay away from overthinking?
answer: The past becomes difficult to leave when we keep treating it as a problem to solve instead of an experience to understand. Overthinking often survives because the mind believes one more replay will produce a different outcome. It won't. Reflection gives lessons; repetition only keeps the wound open. I've explored this pattern more deeply in my writing on my profile if you want to look further. The goal is not to erase the past, but to stop letting yesterday demand a daily explanation.
